description_long: This structure is a large, hemispherical building with a smooth, grid-patterned dome, characteristic of a planetarium. The dome is set atop a flat, dark rectangular base, and features a prominent arched entrance tunnel at the front, which is large enough for a person to walk through. The surface of the dome is uniformly white and appears to be constructed from modular segments, giving it a quilted appearance. On either side of the dome, there are small, cylindrical extensions that may serve as auxiliary entrances or service areas. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible signage or decorative elements, emphasizing the geometric purity of the dome. The entrance is the only visible point of access, and the scale of the building suggests it can accommodate a significant number of visitors inside.
description: A large, white, hemispherical dome building with a quilted surface pattern and a prominent arched entrance, set on a dark rectangular base. The structure is minimalistic and modern, with cylindrical side extensions and no visible signage.
description_view_0: The dome is viewed from the rear, showing the smooth, grid-patterned surface and two cylindrical side extensions, with no visible entrance.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the arched entrance tunnel and a human figure for scale, emphasizing the building's accessibility and size.
description_view_2: The dome is seen from the back, similar to view 0, with the cylindrical side extensions visible but no entrance in sight.
description_view_3: The front-right angle shows both the arched entrance and part of the right cylindrical extension, providing a sense of depth and scale.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance features a large, arched tunnel with a smooth, rounded opening, seamlessly integrated into the dome's surface and lacking any visible doors or decorative elements.
